categories.name: 3-Bet & Defend
categories.blurb: Facing a raise: fold, call, or 3-bet.

categories.name: C-Betting
categories.blurb: Continuation betting the flop with initiative.

categories.name: Turn Barrels
categories.blurb: Firing the second bullet on the turn.

categories.name: River Bluff-Catching
categories.blurb: Hero-calling and value spots on the river.

categories.name: Pot Odds Math
categories.blurb: Do you have the price to continue?

drills.prompt: Folded to you on the Button with A♠Q♦, 100bb. Action?
drills.explain: AQo is a clear open from the Button — it's well inside any sensible button range and plays great in position.

drills.prompt: UTG with K♣J♦, 100bb, folded to you. Action?
drills.explain: KJo is too loose to open from under the gun at a 6-max table — it's frequently dominated by the hands that continue against an early-position raise.

drills.prompt: Cutoff with T♠9♠, folded to you. Action?
drills.explain: Suited connectors like T9s are standard cutoff opens — great playability, flush and straight potential, and fold equity against the blinds.

drills.prompt: UTG with 7♣2♦ (the worst hand in poker). Action?
drills.explain: 72o is the classic auto-fold. There is no position or stack depth where opening this for value makes sense.

drills.prompt: BB with 9♥8♥ on 7♠6♣2♦. Villain bets 4 into 8 (you must call 4 to win 12). You have an open-ended straight draw. Call or fold?
drills.explain: You're getting 3:1, needing ~25% equity. An open-ender has ~31% to improve by the river plus extra equity from overcards and backdoors — an easy call.

drills.prompt: You have the nut flush draw (A♥5♥) on K♥9♥2♣. Villain overbets 15 into 20. Call or fold by the math?
drills.explain: You need 15/(35+15) ≈ 43% to call. A bare flush draw is only ~35% to hit by the river — without enough implied odds, the immediate price is too steep. (With strong implied odds this becomes closer.)

drills.prompt: You raised pre and it's checked to you on K♠7♦2♥ with A♦A♣. Action?
drills.explain: A dry, ace-high/king-high board massively favors the preflop raiser. Bet small at high frequency for value and protection — you want to build the pot with the best hand.

drills.prompt: You hold A♣Q♦ on 9♥8♥7♠ (very wet) as the preflop raiser, checked to you. Best high-frequency line?
drills.explain: On low, connected, two-tone boards the caller's range connects hard while your overcards have little equity. Checking back controls the pot and protects your checking range.

drills.prompt: Top pair A♠J♣ on A♥9♦4♣7♠2♥. Villain bets 10 into 30 on the river. Call or fold?
drills.explain: You're getting 4:1 and only need ~20% equity to call. Top pair good kicker beats plenty of missed draws and worse aces — you only need to be right one time in five.

drills.prompt: K♥Q♥ on J♥5♥2♣8♠ — you have the nut flush draw + two overcards, checked to you on the turn. Action?
drills.explain: A huge combo draw (nut flush draw + overcards ≈ 15 outs) is a premium semi-bluff. Barrel to deny equity and set up a river shove — you have fold equity now and can improve to the nuts.
